skill.name: security-scanning-security-hardening
skill.description: Coordinate multi-layer security scanning and hardening across application, infrastructure, and compliance controls.
skill.summary: Plan and coordinate defense-in-depth security assessments, remediation, validation, monitoring, and compliance checks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many high-severity keywords, but most are false positives caused by defensive security terminology in SKILL.md. The confirmed risk is dual-use guidance for penetration testing with Metasploit and custom exploits, which requires authorization and careful human oversight.

medium_findings.title: Dual-use penetration testing guidance
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs a security-auditor agent to use Burp Suite, Metasploit, and custom exploits during penetration testing. This is legitimate in authorized assessments, but it can enable offensive activity if used against systems without permission.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The named tools and exploit language are directly present in the penetration testing phase. The surrounding context is defensive validation, so the concern is dual-use rather than confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static weak-cryptography detections are false positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts at lines 3, 50, 51, 58, and 140 do not identify use of weak crypto. These lines describe security hardening, encryption review, vulnerability remediation, and workflow coordination.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain no DES, MD5, SHA1, RC4, or similar weak algorithm instruction. They are normal security assessment language.

low_findings.title: Static C2 detections are compliance acronym false positives
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword alerts at lines 110 and 122 refer to SOC2 compliance requirements and configuration options. No command-and-control behavior, beaconing, or remote operator workflow is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is SOC2, a compliance framework. There is no semantic evidence of C2 infrastructure or malicious control channels.

low_findings.title: Static storage and jailbreak detections are defensive mobile controls
low_findings.description: Line 76 mentions secure local storage with encryption and root or jailbreak detection as mobile hardening measures. This is not browser storage abuse or a prompt injection attempt.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is inside a mobile security hardening section and describes protective controls. I found no instruction to override system prompts or bypass AI safety rules.

low_findings.title: Static reconnaissance detection is safety guidance
low_findings.description: Line 31 tells users to avoid intrusive testing in production without approval. This reduces operational risk and does not instruct unauthorized reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is framed as a safety restriction. It does not provide reconnaissance commands, targets, or data collection steps.

dangerous_patterns.title: Exploit tooling referenced in prompt instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill names Metasploit and custom exploits as part of a penetration testing validation workflow. This should remain limited to authorized systems and controlled test environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exploit tooling reference is explicit, but the surrounding phase validates implemented defenses. This supports a medium dual-use rating instead of a critical malicious rating.

content.value_statement: Security work often spans code, infrastructure, monitoring, and compliance teams. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for coordinated assessment, remediation, validation, and reporting.
actual_capabilities: Organizes security work into assessment, remediation, control implementation, and validation phases.
Guides vulnerability scanning across SAST, DAST, dependency audits, secrets detection, and SBOM generation.
Coordinates threat modeling, architecture review, backend hardening, frontend hardening, and mobile hardening tasks.
Defines security control areas for authentication, authorization, infrastructure, secrets management, and monitoring.
Includes compliance verification prompts for OWASP ASVS, CIS, SOC2, privacy controls, HIPAA, and PCI-DSS.
Specifies expected deliverables such as risk matrices, remediation reports, SIEM configurations, and audit evidence.
limitations: It is a planning and orchestration guide, not a standalone scanner or enforcement tool.
It requires authorized scope, test environments, and qualified security review before invasive testing.
It depends on external tools and agents that must be configured separately.
It does not guarantee compliance certification or complete vulnerability removal.

use_cases.title: Prepare a DevSecOps hardening plan
use_cases.description: Create a phased security program that connects scanning, remediation, controls, monitoring, and compliance evidence.

use_cases.title: Coordinate remediation across product teams
use_cases.description: Translate vulnerability and threat model results into backend, frontend, mobile, and infrastructure hardening tasks.

use_cases.title: Validate controls before an audit
use_cases.description: Use structured validation prompts to collect evidence for OWASP, CIS, SOC2, privacy, HIPAA, or PCI-DSS checks.

prompt_templates.title: Start a security baseline
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin with an authorized project and a limited scope.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a security baseline plan for [project]. Scope only [systems]. Include scanning types, expected outputs, and approval checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize remediation work
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn existing findings into a focused action plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to prioritize these security findings for [application]. Group actions by risk, affected team, validation method, and rollback requirement.

prompt_templates.title: Design layered controls
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan defense-in-depth controls for a service or platform.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design hardening controls for [system]. Cover identity, network, API, secrets, logging, monitoring, and compliance requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Run pre-audit validation
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare evidence for a formal security or compliance review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a validation and evidence plan for [frameworks]. Include control owners, test methods, artifacts, and residual risk notes.

output_examples.input: Create a security hardening roadmap for a SaaS API before SOC2 review.
output: A four-phase roadmap covering baseline assessment, remediation, control implementation, and validation.
A prioritized list of owners for API security, secrets management, cloud controls, and SIEM monitoring.
A compliance evidence checklist mapped to SOC2 and OWASP control areas.

output_examples.input: Prioritize findings from SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, and secrets detection.
output: A risk-ranked remediation queue using severity, exploitability, business impact, and affected assets.
Recommended validation steps for each issue category.
Rollback and monitoring notes for high-impact changes.

output_examples.input: Plan validation after backend, frontend, and mobile hardening.
output: A test plan for authentication, authorization, API security, client-side controls, and mobile storage protections.
Evidence expectations for penetration testing, regression testing, and control effectiveness.
A residual risk summary for issues that need human review.

best_practices: Define written authorization, scope, and test windows before any scanning or penetration testing.
Run invasive validation in staging or approved test environments before production changes.
Track every finding to an owner, remediation decision, validation result, and residual risk note.
anti_patterns: Do not use exploit tooling against systems outside the approved scope.
Do not apply hardening changes without rollback plans and operational owners.
Do not treat generated compliance notes as a substitute for formal audit review.

faq.question: Does this skill perform scans by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides prompts and coordination steps for security agents and external tools.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production systems?
faq.answer: Yes, but only with explicit approval, scoped test plans, maintenance windows, and rollback procedures.

faq.question: Why is this skill rated medium risk?
faq.answer: It includes legitimate dual-use penetration testing guidance, including exploit tooling, so it needs authorized use.

faq.question: Can it help with compliance preparation?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes verification steps for OWASP, CIS, SOC2, privacy controls, HIPAA, and PCI-DSS.

faq.question: Does it replace a security professional?
faq.answer: No. It supports planning and coordination, but qualified security staff must review scope, findings, and fixes.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
